title: Saudi PQC Implementation Program
entry_mechanism: NCA-led national programme converting Saudi government cryptographic infrastructure to post-quantum standards
executive_summary:
Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) is the urgent replacement of today's encryption algorithms (RSA, ECC) with quantum-resistant alternatives before quantum computers powerful enough to break them become available. NIST finalised its first PQC standards in 2024 (CRYSTALS-Kyber, CRYSTALS-Dilithium, SPHINCS+), and the 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat means adversaries may already be collecting Saudi government encrypted traffic today for future decryption.

Node QT-1 targets Saudi Arabia's national PQC migration: systematically inventorying all Saudi government cryptographic infrastructure, prioritising migration based on data sensitivity and exposure, and implementing PQC algorithms across government communications, financial systems, and critical infrastructure. This is a national security programme, not merely an IT upgrade.

Saudi Arabia's national PQC programme is simultaneously a security imperative (protecting Saudi government data from future quantum attacks) and a technical capability investment (creating Saudi cryptography engineers who understand and can implement quantum-resistant algorithms). NCA leadership is essential — this is a national security programme, not an IT project.

companies.name: IBM Quantum Safe
companies.rationale: IBM has the most mature PQC implementation programme and NIST PQC standard contributions. IBM Quantum Safe programme for Saudi government migration.
